summary: `drafts list` must send {filter:{type:"draft"}} to /v3/userdata.getThreads; an empty {} body is rejected with HTTP 400 by the backend's BackendListFetcher.
reason: drafts_list.go shipped an empty {} request body, so `drafts list` 400s against live Superhuman, while its sibling threads_list.go (which sends {filter:{type}}, the PATCH(U3) lesson) succeeds. The getThreads endpoint requires a filter.type discriminator; a regen must preserve that for the drafts path too.
validated_outcome: Live A/B against a real Superhuman account: released binary -> HTTP 400 {"code":400}; patched binary -> 200 with the full draft threadList. Regression test drafts_list_test.go asserts the request carries filter.type=draft against a fake backend that 400s without it.
